feedback.markdownBody:
# Review CHANGES REQUESTED for PAN-1862

# Review Synthesis — PAN-1862 — 2026-06-17T13:05:00Z

## Verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED — schema SCHEMA_VERSION downgraded 56→54 causes reviewer_verdicts migration to never run on existing installs, crashing all review-status upserts on upgrade

## Context
- Manifest: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1862/.pan/review/agent-pan-1862-review-3beb332a/context.json
- Branch: feature/pan-1862
- Workspace: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1862
- HEAD reviewed: 3beb332ae6635ebfdd7a44b089f6217962e929d3
- Cycle number: 1
- Prior cycle SHA: none

## Convoy Status
| Sub-role | Signal | Output | Blocking findings |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| security | ready | .pan/review/agent-pan-1862-review-3beb332a/security.md | 0 |
| correctness | ready | .pan/review/agent-pan-1862-review-3beb332a/correctness.md | 2 |
| performance | ready | .pan/review/agent-pan-1862-review-3beb332a/performance.md | 0 |
| requirements | ready | .pan/review/agent-pan-1862-review-3beb332a/requirements.md | 1 |

## Blocking Findings

### [correctness] BUG-1: reviewer_verdicts column never migrated on upgrade from v56 — `src/lib/database/schema.ts` / `sync-sources/hooks/record-cost-event.js`

**Severity:** MUST
**Scope:** in_pr_scope (both files are in the changeset)

`SCHEMA_VERSION` was downgraded from 56 (current `main`) to 54. The `reviewer_verdicts TEXT` column is added only inside an `if (currentVersion < 54)` migration block. Every existing installation has `user_version = 56`; for those installs `56 < 54` is false so the migration block is skipped, but the version is still written as 54. The `reviewer_verdicts` column is never added.

`upsertReviewStatusSync` unconditionally references `reviewer_verdicts` in the INSERT and `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET` clauses. On any installation upgrading from main (v56), SQLite throws `table review_status has no column named reviewer_verdicts` on the first pipeline event after upgrade — crashing every review/test/merge lifecycle transition, not just the new convoy features.

The same defect is replicated in `sync-sources/hooks/record-cost-event.js` (also downgraded to v54).

**Fix:** Bump `SCHEMA_VERSION` to 57 and add an `if (currentVersion < 57)` migration block that runs `ALTER TABLE review_status ADD COLUMN reviewer_verdicts TEXT`. Apply the same fix to the bundled hook.

---

### [correctness] BUG-2: Dead-fallback reviewers never spawned in re-review — synthesis hangs indefinitely — `src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts`

**Severity:** MUST
**Scope:** in_pr_scope (new `tryResumeConvoyReviewers` code introduced by this PR)

`tryResumeConvoyReviewers` categorises in-scope reviewers into `resumed` (session alive, message sent), `outOfScope` (carried forward), and `deadFallback` (in-scope but session dead). When at least one reviewer was resumed, the function returns `{ success: true, message: "... (fresh-spawn needed: [...])" }` and annotates that dead-fallback reviewers need fresh spawning. The caller treats any non-null return as "done" and exits without spawning those reviewers.

Concrete hang: 2 of 4 reviewer sessions die between review cycles. `tryResumeConvoyReviewers` resumes the 2 alive sessions and returns success. The 2 dead-fallback reviewers are never spawned. The synthesis agent waits for 4 terminal signals; 2 never arrive. Deacon has no `reviewDeadlineAt` for the unspawned reviewers, so no REVIEWER_TIMEOUT is emitted. Recovery requires operator intervention.

The comment in `tryResumeConvoyReviewers` reads "Dead-fallback reviewers will be handled by the caller via fresh spawn" but the caller implements no such handling.

**Fix:** Either (a) `tryResumeConvoyReviewers` returns `deadFallback` to the caller and the caller spawns fresh sessions for those sub-roles before returning, or (b) `tryResumeConvoyReviewers` spawns dead-fallback reviewers itself.

---

### [requirements] FR-6: Per-reviewer verdicts write path absent — `specialists.ts` done-handler / `roles/review.md`

**Severity:** ! (MUST) — Scope: in_pr_scope
**Requirement:** FR-6 / WI-D1 — "Synthesis records a per-sub-role verdict (passed|blocked, with the reviewed commit and a findings pointer) into review status."

The `ReviewStatus.reviewerVerdicts` field, `reviewer_verdicts TEXT` DB column, and associated types all exist. No code path writes these verdicts:
- The `pan admin specialists done review` handler snapshots `reviewedAtCommit` but has no `reviewerVerdicts` parameter path.
- `roles/review.md` synthesis instructions do not include a per-reviewer verdict persistence step.
- `tryResumeConvoyReviewers` reads `existingStatus?.reviewerVerdicts ?? {}` and finds `{}` on every first re-review.

**Cascading impact:** (1) `scope=blockers` (FR-7 config) resolves `reviewersToRerun` from an always-empty verdicts map → returns `[]` → `tryResumeConvoyReviewers` returns `null` → falls back to kill-all respawn, defeating the purpose of the `blockers` scope entirely. (2) FR-8 (`scope=changed`) carries forward empty verdicts — the RE-REVIEW message to synthesis always has no useful carried-forward data. (3) FR-9's synthesis re-arm depends on populated verdicts to know which sub-roles to merge; without them the feature is a stub.

**Fix:** Either (a) extend `pan admin specialists done review` with `--reviewer-verdict <subRole>=passed|blocked:<commitSha>` flags and persist via `setReviewStatusSync`, or (b) add instructions to `roles/review.md` telling synthesis to write per-reviewer verdicts directly (e.g. via `pan admin specialists set-reviewer-verdict`) before signaling the final verdict.

## Non-blocking Findings

### [security] ~ config.yaml chmod removal leaves API keys re-exposed on pre-existing loose-permission files — `src/lib/config-yaml.ts`

The PR removes two explicit `chmodSync`/`chmodAsync(0o600)` calls (one per write path) that enforced `config.yaml` file permissions after every write. The deleted comment explicitly explained why: `writeFileSync`'s `mode` option applies only on creation, not on overwrites of pre-existing files with wider permissions. `config.yaml` is the only secrets-bearing file in the codebase that no longer enforces this; all peers (`pi-codex-auth.ts`, `internal-token.ts`, `pty-token.ts`, `cliproxy.ts`) still use `chmodSync` or `{ mode: 0o600 }` post-write. Attack path requires a prior widening of permissions (CI runner, shared machine, debugging `chmod a+r`), but the regression from prior behavior is real. Advisory: restore the two `chmodSync`/`chmodAsync(0o600)` calls.

### [security] ? reviewedAtCommit shell interpolation without format assertion — `src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts:367`

`reviewedAtCommit` from SQLite is interpolated into `git diff --name-only ${reviewedAtCommit} HEAD`. In practice always a 40-char hex SHA, but no format assertion exists at the call site. Requires local DB write access; local-only tool, low severity. Defense-in-depth fix: validate against `/^[0-9a-f]{4,40}$/i` before interpolation, or switch to `execFile` with an argument array.

### [correctness] BUG-3 · SHOULD — `_cacheMissNotified` never cleared across review cycles — `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts`

Module-level `Set` keyed on `agentId` alone; reviewer session names are reused across re-review cycles. After the first cycle's cache-miss notification, all subsequent cycles for the same issue are silently suppressed — the notification system only fires once per agentId ever. Diagnostic impact only (FR-12 alerting). Fix: key on `(agentId, reviewRunId)` or clear entries when a reviewer session is killed.

### [correctness] BUG-4 · SHOULD — Pi session path resolution removed without verified fallback — `src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts`

The explicit `resolvePiSessionPath` branch (which encapsulated the PAN-1908 knowledge that pi/kimi agents write JSONL to the agent-directory root rather than `sessions/`) was removed. Pi agents now fall through to the unified session-id lookup chain. Whether that chain covers root-level JSONL is not verifiable from the diff alone. If it does not, loading any pi/kimi work-agent transcript would silently return empty or 404 — a regression from PAN-1908. Recommend adding a targeted integration test or direct verification before merging.

### [performance] ~ Serial `isPaneDead` calls in re-review resume loop — `src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts:405`

4 sequential `tmux display-message` subprocess invocations in the `tryResumeConvoyReviewers` loop (~40–80 ms serial latency on the stale-runId re-review path). Parallelizable via `Promise.all` before the loop.

### [performance] ~ Full JSONL read ×4 in `handleDiscoveryReady` — `src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts:1115`

`forkSession({ fullHistory: true })` is called 4× concurrently, each reading the same source JSONL independently. Peak memory = 4× source file size (~20–120 MB for typical-to-large diffs). Fix: read and sanitize once, write 4 destinations concurrently.

### [performance] ≉ Sync I/O per patrol tick for cache-miss detection — `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:6071`

`readFileSync` + synchronous SQLite query per unnotified reviewer sub-role per patrol tick. Bounded at 4 reviewers × N active issues × 60 s cadence; negligible at low concurrency. Perceptible only at very high concurrent review load (10+ simultaneous issues).

### [requirements] ~ FR-9 synthesis re-arm lacks role-template guidance

`roles/review.md` has no `## Re-review re-arm` section explaining how synthesis should process a `RE-REVIEW:` message (merge carried verdicts with new results, wait only for in-scope signals). The server sends a well-formed message but the template-spec contract is underspecified. Capable models will likely follow the message content; advisory: add a dedicated section to `roles/review.md`.

### [requirements] ~ FR-17 model-uniformity banner gated on sub-role expansion

The model-uniformity banner is inside the `canExpand && isExpanded` block — only visible when operators expand the review sub-roles. Operators who never expand sub-roles will not see the warning. Advisory: coherent with the spec's "near the sub-role pickers" requirement; flag for future improvement.

## Blocking Summary

3 blocking findings, all in-PR-scope:

| # | Sub-role | Severity | File(s) | Summary |
|---|----------|----------|---------|---------|
| 1 | correctness | MUST | `schema.ts`, `record-cost-event.js` | SCHEMA_VERSION 56→54 downgrade causes reviewer_verdicts migration to never run on existing installs → crashes all review-status upserts |
| 2 | correctness | MUST | `review-agent.ts` | Dead-fallback reviewers never spawned in re-review; synthesis hangs indefinitely |
| 3 | requirements | MUST | `specialists.ts`, `roles/review.md` | FR-6 write path absent; per-reviewer verdicts never populated; scope=blockers and carried-verdict features non-functional |

## Clean Sub-roles

**Performance** — no blocking findings. All new I/O paths are on bounded, non-hot code paths.

**Security** — no blocking findings. No injection vulnerabilities, auth bypasses, XSS surfaces, SSRF paths, or committed secrets in the changed code.

Source: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1862/.pan/review/agent-pan-1862-review-3beb332a/synthesis.md

## Required action

Fix every blocking review finding, commit the fixes, then re-request review with:

`pan review request PAN-1862 -m "Fixed review issues"`
